response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has a broad and impressive polyglot background with genuine distributed systems exposure (Dunnhumby's high-throughput microservices) and strong TDD culture, but he critically fails the role's hard minimum qualifications. The position requires 8 years of C++ experience as a baseline; his resume shows roughly 6 months of professional C++ work at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor's thesis project, with C++ absent entirely from his listed skills. His primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang. Additionally, he lacks the required 5 years of large-scale cloud infrastructure or compute platform work, has no demonstrated technical leadership of cloud or DevOps engineering teams, and holds only a Bachelor's degree with no preference-matching postgraduate credential. The role is a poor match at nearly every minimum qualification level.
strengths: Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency DLang services
Strong, consistent testing and software craftsmanship culture across multiple roles (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, public workshops)
Breadth across embedded, web, mobile, and infrastructure domains shows adaptability and system-level thinking
concerns: C++ experience is approximately 6 months professional (NI, 2014–2015) versus the 8-year hard minimum; C++ is not even listed in his skills section
No cloud compute platform or large-scale infrastructure ownership — AWS CDK usage at Optilyz and Kubernetes self-hosting at GISCollective do not constitute production cloud architecture leadership
No formal technical leadership role managing or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers; staff-level titles reflect individual contribution, not team-level technical leadership
